questions.question: Explain the concept of cache coherence in multicore processors. Describe the MESI protocol and how it maintains data consistency across cores.
keyPoints: Cache coherence ensures multiple cores see the same value for the same memory location
MESI protocol has four states: Modified, Exclusive, Shared, Invalid
Bus snooping or directory-based coherence mechanisms

questions.question: Describe the different memory types in GPU architecture. How do global memory, shared memory, and registers differ in terms of access latency, scope, and programming model?
keyPoints: Global memory: high latency, all threads can access, off-chip
Shared memory: low latency, within a thread block, on-chip
Constant and texture caches for read-only data access patterns

questions.question: Discuss heterogeneous computing and explain how CPUs and GPUs collaborate in modern systems. Provide examples of workloads suitable for CPU execution versus GPU acceleration. Also explain the concept of NUMA architecture in this context.
keyPoints: Heterogeneous computing combines CPUs (control-oriented) and GPUs (data-parallel) for efficiency
CPU suitable for: sequential tasks, branching logic, low-latency operations
GPU suitable for: massively parallel tasks (graphics, ML, scientific computing)
NUMA (Non-Uniform Memory Access) affects memory locality in multi-socket systems

questions.question: Explain the thread synchronization mechanisms available in CUDA/OpenCL programming. How does the SIMT (Single Instruction Multiple Thread) execution model work in GPUs? Discuss the challenges involved in porting CPU applications to GPU using OpenCL with code examples.
keyPoints: SIMT: Warps execute same instruction on different data, divergence reduces efficiency
Thread synchronization: __syncthreads() in CUDA, barrier() in OpenCL
OpenCL provides platform, device, context, and kernel abstraction
Challenges: memory transfer overhead, branch divergence, limited atomic operations
